nodes.title: For this particular template, we're going to use prompts.
Move through each step to explore how prompts and nodes help guide the process.
Collect evidence from real users before mapping the workflow.

✍️ Add quotes, pain points, observations, and real behaviors.


🤔 Focus on what users actually do, not what they say they want.


📝 Cluster insights to identify themes.
Work requests arrive through multiple channels, causing missed details and duplicated effort
Priority definitions vary by team or role, creating constant rework and frustration
Lack of visibility makes it hard to understand ownership, status, and dependencies
“I’m not sure if this was already asked in Slack or email”
“Everything is urgent until someone higher up says it’s not”
“I only find out something is blocked when it’s already late”
Team members manually copy requests from Slack or email into task tools
Frequent sync meetings are used just to clarify priorities and ownership
People create personal to-do lists because the system feels unreliable
Jira or Asana used inconsistently across teams
Shared spreadsheets to track priorities or approvals
Slack threads pinned or starred as temporary reminders
Capture all incoming work in one place without losing context
Clearly understand what needs attention now vs later
Coordinate work across teams with minimal meetings
👉On the left toolbar top, you’ll see the sparkle icon. That’s the AI menu. Click it.

👉 A panel, called Agile Coaching opens on the right.

That’s where you drop your prompt.
👉 Paste the following prompt in the chat:

Analyze the insights by using the five categories in the template (Pain Points, Key Quotes, Observed Behaviors, Current Tools / Workarounds, User Goals).
Group the insights into themes and explain what connects them.
For each theme, output a table with:Theme Name
Related Pain Points
Supporting Quotes
Observed Behaviors
Tools/Workarounds Mentioned
Jobs-to-be-done User Goals
Potential Impact
This summary will be used to build a workflow diagram next, so make the themes clear and actionable.
Output a table."
nodes.body: Summarize the research insights into themes. Output a table with Pain Point, Frequency, Evidence (quote), and Impact.

nodes.title: 👉 Evaluate the result, and if everything looks good, hit Add to canvas and place it in the frame on this right.
Map the real current workflow and expose friction.

Use the research insights to create a step-by-step workflow showing how users complete their tasks. Identify and highlight any bottlenecks, delays, or high-friction steps. Output in simple flowchart language or Mermaid syntax.
nodes.body: Summarize the research insights into themes. Output a table with Pain Point, Frequency, Evidence (quote), and Impact.

nodes.title: 👉 In the Agile Coach chat, paste the following prompt.
👉Select the Summary table generate in Step 1.
👉 Evaluate the result, and if everything looks good, hit Add to canvas and place it in the frame below.
Design UI screens that directly solve workflow gaps.
In Tools, Media and Integrations, find the tool called Prototype and run it. It will open a chat like Agile Coaching.

👋 Hi! I’m Dexter.

I build AI products and rollups helping service businesses scale.

Visit www.theportfoliopath.com to join my newsletter.

This template is designed to guide you from research insights to a functional prototype. It combines structured research documentation, workflow visualization, and prototyping, all in one collaborative space.

Collect and organize evidence from real users, including quotes, pain points, behaviors, and current workarounds.


Use the sticky notes provided, grouped into categories.

Translate insights into a visual workflow using swimlanes, flowcharts, or Mermaid diagrams.


Map each step, decision point, and interaction along the workflow. Highlight bottlenecks, delays, or friction points identified in research.

Turn your workflow and insights into a clickable or visual prototype.

Break the workflow into screens or states, linking them according to user actions.
Users are unable to filter the menu by category on mobile. Reports started coming in from customers yesterday .
Investigate why the categry filter isn't working on mobile
I think there might ab bt be a bug with the mobile category filter. I can't get it to work ! @jenny can you take a look? "
Fix the issue so filtering by category works consistently
Verify the fix on latest iOS and Android
I think there might be a bug with the mobile category filter. I can't get it to work! @jenny can you take a look?
I'll check it out. Can you tell me which category specifically is causing problems?
Task will be created in Relay and linked to this Slack conversation
3 new task assignments are ready for your review
Users are unable to filter by category on mobile
Lead qualification alerts not triggering in CRM
Weekly marketing reports not generating on time
Need access to new employee onboarding system
I think there might be a bug with the mobile category filter. I can't get it to work! @jenny can you take a look?
Users are unable to filter the menu by category on mobile. Reports started coming in from customers yesterday. This is affecting all mobile users on both iOS and Android platforms.
Based on the content analysis, most Product team requests are being assigned to Jenny Lee (90% match). Consider updating your routing rules to automatically assign similar requests to her team.
3 new task assignments are ready for your review

The mobile app notifications system is experiencing intermittent failures. Users report missing important alerts, particularly for message replies and task assignments. Engineering team needs to conduct a comprehensive audit of the notification system to identify failure points and resolve the issues.
I think there might be a bug with the mobile notifications system. I can't get any alerts for replies to my messages! @jenny can you take a look?
I'm seeing this too. Let me check the logs. Are you on iOS or Android?
iOS, latest version. I'm also hearing from several users on both platforms with the same issue.
This is concerning. Let's make this a priority task. We need to conduct a full audit of the notification system and fix it ASAP.
I'm creating a task for this now. Will assign it to the mobile team and start investigating right away. I'll share updates as we make progress.
Identify all notification failure points in both iOS and Android apps
Document notification delivery success rates across different device types
Verify push notification registration process is working correctly
Test notification delivery for all event types (messages, assignments, reminders)
Review notification logs for past 7 days
Test notification delivery on different iOS versions
Verify the fix on latest iOS and Android

Initial log analysis shows approximately 32% of notifications are failing to deliver. The issue appears to be more prevalent on iOS devices. Will continue investigating the API calls to identify the exact failure point.
Thanks for the update, David. I've allocated additional resources to help with testing. @Priya can you focus on iOS device testing? Let's aim to have preliminary findings by tomorrow.
3 new task assignments are ready for your review
Track and monitor the status of all your submitted requests
Marketing team needs updated navigation to match new branding
Dashboard shows error when accessing sales metrics
Transfer customer data to new CRM system
Identified the issue with the payment API. Working on a fix now and should have a patch ready for testing by end of day.
Wireframes for the new navigation have been completed. I've uploaded them to the shared drive and would appreciate your feedback before moving to the next stage.
Fixed the authentication issue that was preventing the dashboard from loading. The system should now correctly authenticate all users and display the sales metrics without any errors.
Task Detail View with Embedded Context
Place the prototype in the board on the bottom.

Click Create from screenshot. This opens a new window where you can work on the prototype. On the right, you’ll see the Sidekick chat. When prompted with “Where would you like to start?”, select A screenshot.
When prompted with “Select the device type that the prototype is for”, choose the format that fits your use case. In our case, we selected Desktop.
Click Choose file and upload the screenshot of your software prototype.
Sidekick will then generate the base of the prototype for you. When it’s ready, click Add to canvas, then Back to canvas.
Click the Prototype button again and place it on the board to the bottom.

Click Create with AI to open the Sidekick chat. Then select the Summary from Step 1, the Mermaid Diagram from Step 2, and the prototype you just created, and enter the following prompt into the Prototype chat:
Based on the workflow and pain points, suggest UI improvements or additional screens to reduce friction.
nodes.body: Summarize the research insights into themes. Output a table with Pain Point, Frequency, Evidence (quote), and Impact.
